acceptance_targets: [chapter_02_craft_001] Reading the revised passage from snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:141 through the burn sequence beginning at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:257, a reviewer must be able to identify a distinct dramatic function for each remaining stop on first read: one demonstrates unstable heat, one introduces a genuinely new category of consequence or forces a visible change in Silas's behavior, and the Nora/Ettie room escalates into immediate injury. If the middle can still be summarized as three consecutive versions of 'another household explains why bridelight is bad,' it fails.

[FBR-001] Pass if snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:502 no longer contains a self-announcing hinge and the shift is legible through concrete reaction in the surrounding lines; fail if the prose still tells the reader that the beat has arrived instead of dramatizing it.

findings.acceptance_test: Reading the revised passage from snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:141 through the burn sequence beginning at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:257, a reviewer must be able to identify a distinct dramatic function for each remaining stop on first read: one demonstrates unstable heat, one introduces a genuinely new category of consequence or forces a visible change in Silas's behavior, and the Nora/Ettie room escalates into immediate injury. If the middle can still be summarized as three consecutive versions of 'another household explains why bridelight is bad,' it fails.
findings.problem: The second scene proves the same point too many times in the same shape before it escalates. Mrs. Rusk, Bess, and Nora are all presented as sequential witness rooms where Inez brings Silas inside, a household worker names a practical cost, and Silas absorbs another rebuke. Because only the Nora/Ettie room turns the demonstration into immediate bodily consequence, the earlier two stops flatten into a procedural case-study march instead of a steadily tightening confrontation.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the household-tour sequence anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:141, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:179, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:217 so each stop changes the chapter's pressure rather than restating it. Compress or merge one of the first two rooms, or give the Bess passage beginning at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:179 a new turning function that materially alters Silas's stance before the Nora/Ettie escalation beginning at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_02.md:217.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:502 no longer contains a self-announcing hinge and the shift is legible through concrete reaction in the surrounding lines; fail if the prose still tells the reader that the beat has arrived instead of dramatizing it.
findings.problem: Full-book explanatory hinge pattern ('There it was' / 'That landed') appears in 13 chapters. This chapter's line at 502 announces the beat from above the scene instead of letting Inez's formality and the landing's social pressure do the work.
findings.rewrite_direction: REPLACE the hinge at snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:502. Let Silas register Inez's 'do not' through bodily pressure, the women watching him, or the altered feel of the tray in his hand; do not swap in another summary flag like 'that was the moment.' Keep chapter_03's cleaner early instance as the lone retained version if you want one anywhere in the book.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Prioritize structural and craft blockers first: causality, chapter architecture, stakes clarity, continuity logic, and all HIGH/CRITICAL defects.
revision_pass.label: HIGH/CRITICAL structural/craft fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: Lean into the book's strange civic biology, vulgar humor, erotic consequence, and class abrasion. When a choice appears between the cleaner version and the truer, messier one, choose the messier version so long as causality and character truth remain exact. Treat the city's slime, smell, heat, and appetite as part of the novel's beauty rather than embarrassment to be polished away.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If sex, shame, hunger, class humiliation, bodily mess, or economic pressure starts drifting into decorous vagueness, rewrite until the scene carries concrete sensory and social cost on the page.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the norm across the novel; uncontracted forms should signal emphasis, class performance, ceremonial language, or Gideon's deliberate control.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate crowd scenes with cleanup, ritual with labor, public humiliation with private tenderness, and single-sweep chapters with multi-scene accumulations; no two consecutive chapters should feel built from the same emotional weather, dialogue density, or scene rhythm.
prose_style_profile.diction: Period-aware but not museum-stiff; vivid, bodily, class-conscious, and willing to be ugly or funny without slipping into arch parody or modern snark.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: close third person anchored to Silas Vale for the full novel, with no head-hopping and no neutral historian voice; Inez remains legible through observed action, dialogue, and the objects she shares.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Elastic and pressure-sensitive: quick comic snap in public scenes, thicker sensory accumulation in labor and erotic passages, and hard clean sentences at moments of shame or decision.
